name: DES-2B "Dervish"
description: The Dervish refines the Sandstorm's electrostatic separation with resonant frequency tuning — the triboelectric charging tunnel oscillates at frequencies matched to the crystallographic structure of common ice polymorphs, preferentially charging ice-bearing grains ten times more efficiently than random-collision charging. Four centrifugal drums operate in a two-stage parallel-series configuration: two coarse drums feed two fine-recovery drums, and each pair shares a common vibro-fluidized bed pre-stage. The resonant charging system enables reliable extraction even from sites below 3% water ice where earlier sifting designs plateaued. At 40 tonnes and 2.0 MW, the Dervish is a substantial facility component, but its 472 kg/hr water output makes it the backbone of mid-game dry-site operations.
